author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xvii. 19, ‘Why could not we cast it out’? etc., and on prayer.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xvii. 19, ‘Why could not we cast it out’? etc., and on prayer.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our Lord Jesus Christ reproved unbelief even in His own disciples, as we heard just now when the Gospel was being read.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when they had said, “Why could not we cast him out?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He answered, “Because of your unbelief.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the Apostles were unbelievers, who is a believer?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What must the lambs do, if the rams totter?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet the mercy of the Lord did not disdain them in their unbelief; but reproved, nourished, perfected, crowned them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they themselves, as mindful of their own weakness, said to Him, as we read in a certain place in the Gospel, “Lord, increase our faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lord,” say they, “increase our faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The knowing that they had a deficiency, was the first advantage; a greater happiness still, to know who it was of whom they were asking.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lord,” say they, “increase our faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, if they did not bring their hearts as it were to the fountain, and knocked that that might be opened to them, out of which they might fill them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He would that men should knock at Him, not that He might repel those that knock, but that He might exercise those who long.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For do you think, Brethren, that God doth not know what is needful for you?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He knoweth and preventeth our desires, who knoweth our want.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so when He taught His disciples to pray, and warned them not to use many words in prayer, He saith, “Use not many words; for your Father knoweth what things ye have need of before ye ask Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now the Lord saith something different from this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because He misliked that we should use many words in prayer, He said to us, “When ye pray, use not many words; for your Father knoweth what things ye have need of before ye ask Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If our “Father knoweth what things we have need of before we ask Him,” why do we use even few words?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the use of prayer at all, if “our Father knoweth” already “what things we have need of”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saith to one, Do not make thy prayer to Me at great length; for I know what is needful for thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If so, Lord, why should I so much as pray at all?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wouldest not that I should use long prayers, yea rather Thou dost even bid me to use near none at all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then what meaneth that precept in another place?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He who saith, “Use not many words in prayer,” saith in another place, “Ask, and it shall be given you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And that thou mightest not think that this first precept to ask was given cursorily, He added, “Seek, and ye shall find.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And that thou mightest not think that this too was cursorily given, see what He added further, see with what He finished.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Knock, and it shall be opened unto you:” see what He added.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He would have thee ask that thou mayest receive, and seek that thou mayest find, and knock that thou mayest enter in.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seeing then that our Father knoweth already what is needful for us, how and why do we ask? why seek? why knock? why weary ourselves in asking, and seeking, and knocking, to instruct Him who knoweth already?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in another place the words of the Lord are, “Men ought always to pray, and not to faint.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If men “ought always to pray,” how doth He say, “Use not many words”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How can I always pray, if I so quickly make an end?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here Thou biddest me to finish quickly; there “always to pray and not to faint:” what doth this mean?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now that thou mayest understand this, “ask, seek, knock.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For for this cause is it closed, not to shut thee out, but to exercise thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, ought we to exhort to prayer, both ourselves and you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For other hope have we none amid the manifold evils of this present world, than to knock in prayer, to believe and to maintain the belief firm in the heart, that thy Father only doth not give thee what He knoweth is not expedient for thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou knowest what thou dost desire; He knoweth what is good for thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Imagine thyself under a physician, and in weak health, as is the very truth; for all this life of ours is a weakness; and a long life is nothing else but a prolonged weakness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Imagine thyself then to be sick under the physician’s hand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast a desire to ask thy physician leave to drink a draught of fresh wine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art not prohibited from asking, for it may chance to do thee no harm, or even good to receive it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not then hesitate to ask; ask, hesitate not; but if thou receive not, do not take it to heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now if thou wouldest act thus in the hands of a man, the physician of the body, how much more in the hands of God, who is the Physician, the Creator, and Restorer, both of thy body and soul?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore, see how the Lord in this passage exhorted His disciples to prayer, when He said, “Ye could not cast out this devil because of your unbelief.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For then exhorting them to prayer He ended thus; “this kind is not cast out but by prayer and fasting.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If a man must pray, to cast out devils from another, how much more to cast out his own covetousness? how much more to cast out his own drunkenness? how much more to cast out his own luxuriousness? how much more to cast out his own uncleanness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How many things in a man are there, which if they are persevered in, allow of no admission into the kingdom of heaven!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider, Brethren, how a physician is entreated for the preservation of temporal health, how, if any one is desperately ill, is he ashamed or slow to throw himself at a man’s feet? to bathe in tears the footsteps of any very able chief physician?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what if the physician say to him, “Thou canst not else be cured, except I bind thee, and use the fire and knife”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He will answer,” Do what thou wilt, only cure me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With what eagerness does he long for the health of a few days, fleeting as a vapour, that for it he is content to be bound, and submit to the fire, and knife, and to be watched, that he neither eat nor drink what, or when, he pleases!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All this he will endure, that he may die a little later; and yet he will not endure ever so little, that he may never die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If God, who is the Heavenly Physician over us, saith to thee, “Wilt thou be cured?” what wouldest thou say but “Yes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or it may be thou wouldest not say so, because thou fanciest thyself to be in health, that is, because thou art more grievously sick.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if we suppose two sick persons, one who implores the physician with tears, the other, who in his sickness with infatuation derides him; he will hold out hope to the one that weeps, and will deplore the case of the other that laughs.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why? but because the sounder in health he thinks himself, the more dangerous his sickness is!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This was the case with the Jews.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ came to them that were sick; He found them all sick.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no one then flatter himself on his healthful state, lest the physician give him up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He found all sick; it is the Apostle’s judgment, “For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Though He found them all sick, yet were there two sorts of sick folk.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The one came to the Physician, clave to Christ, heard, honoured, followed Him, were converted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He received all without disdaining any, for to heal them, who healed of free favour, who cured by Almighty power.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When then He received them, and joined them to Himself to be healed, they rejoiced.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But there was another sort of sick, who had already become infatuated through the sickness of iniquity, and did not know themselves to be sick; they mocked Him, because He received the sick, and said to His disciples, “Lo, what manner of man is your Master, who eateth with publicans and sinners.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He who knew what and who they were answered them, “They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He showed them who the “whole” were, and who the “sick.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I am not come,” He saith, “to call the righteous, but sinners.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If sinners, He would say, do not come to Me, wherefore am I come? for whose sake am I come?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If all are whole, wherefore hath so great a Physician come down from heaven? why hath He prepared for us a medicine not out of His stores, but of His own blood?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That sort of sick then who had a milder sickness, who felt themselves to be sick, clave to the Physician, that they might be healed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they whose sickness was more dangerous mocked the Physician, and abused the sick.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whither did their frenzy proceed at last?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To seize the Physician, bind, scourge, crown Him with thorns, hang Him upon a Tree, kill Him on the Cross!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou marvel?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The sick slew the Physician; but the Physician by being slain healed the frantic patient.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For first, not forgetting on the Cross His own character, and manifesting forth His patience to us, and giving us an example of love to our enemies; as He saw them raging round Him, who had known their disease, seeing He was the Physician, who had known the frenzy by which they had become infatuated, He said at once to the Father, “Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now suppose ye that those Jews were not malignant, cruel, bloody, turbulent, and enemies of the Son of God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Suppose ye that that cry, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do,” was ineffectual and in vain?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saw them all, but He knew amongst them those that should one day be His.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In a word, He died, because it was so expedient, that by His Death He might kill death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God died, that an exchange might be effected by a kind of heavenly contract, that man might not see death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Christ is God, but He died not in that Nature in which He is God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the same Person is God and man; for God and man is one Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The human nature was assumed, that we might be changed for the better; He did not degrade the Divine Nature down to the lower.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He assumed that which He was not, He did not lose that which He was.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forasmuch then as He is both God and man, being pleased that we should live by that which was His, He died in that which was ours.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He had nothing Himself, whereby He could die; nor had we anything whereby we could live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what was He who had nothing whereby He could die?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou seek for anything in God whereby He may die, thou wilt not find it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we all die, who are flesh; men bearing about sinful flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seek out for that whereby sin may live; it hath it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then neither could He have death in that which was His own, nor we life in that which was our own; but we have life from that which is His, He death from what is ours.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What an exchange!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What hath He given, and what received?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Men who trade enter into commercial intercourse for exchange of things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ancient commerce was only an exchange of things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man gave what he had, and received what he had not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For example, he had wheat, but had no barley; another had barley, but no wheat; the former gave the wheat which he had, and received the barley which he had not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How simple it was that the larger quantity should make up for the cheaper sort!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then another man gives barley, to receive wheat; lastly, another gives lead, to receive silver, only he gives much lead against a little silver; another gives wool, to receive a ready-made garment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And who can enumerate all these exchanges?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But no one gives life to receive death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not in vain then was the voice of the Physician as He hung upon the tree.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in order that He might die for us because the Word could not die, “The Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hung upon the Cross, but in the flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There was the meanness, which the Jews despised; there the dearness, by which the Jews were delivered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For for them was it said, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And that voice was not in vain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He died, was buried, rose again, having passed forty days with His disciples, He ascended into heaven, He sent the Holy Ghost on them, who waited for the promise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They were filled with the Holy Ghost, whom they had received, and began to speak with the tongues of all nations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then the Jews who were present, amazed that unlearned and ignorant men, whom they had known as brought up among them with one tongue, should in the Name of Christ speak in all tongues, were in astonishment, and learnt from Peter’s words whence this gift came.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He gave it, who hung upon the tree.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He gave it, who was derided as He hung upon the tree, that from His seat in heaven He might give the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They of whom He had said, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do,” heard, believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They believed, were baptized, and their conversion was effected.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What conversion?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In faith they drank the Blood of Christ, which in fury they had shed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, to finish this discourse with that with which we began it, let us pray, and let us rely on God; let us live as He enjoineth; and when we totter in this life, let us call upon Him as the disciples called, saying, “Lord, increase our faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peter both put his trust in Him, and tottered; but notwithstanding he was not disregarded and left to sink, but was lifted up and raised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For his trust whence was it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not from anything of his own; but from what was the Lord’s.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lord, if it be Thou, bid me come unto Thee on the water.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For on the water was the Lord walking.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If it be Thou, bid me come unto Thee on the water.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I know that if it be Thou, Thou biddest, and it is done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And He saith, Come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He went down at His bidding, but in his own weakness he was afraid.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless when he was afraid, he cried out, “Lord, save me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then the Lord took him by the hand, and said, “O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He first invited him, He delivered him, as he tottered, and stumbled; that it might be fulfilled which was said in the Psalm, “If I said my foot hath slipped, Thy mercy, O Lord, aided me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are then two kinds of blessings, temporal and eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Temporal blessings are health, substance, honour, friends, a home, children, a wife, and the other things of this life in which we are sojourners.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Put we up then in the hostelry of this life as travellers passing on, and not as owners intending to remain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But eternal blessings are, first, eternal life itself, the incorruption and immortality of body and soul, the society of Angels, the heavenly city, glory unfailing, Father and father-land, the former without death, the latter without a foe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These blessings let us desire with all eagerness, let us ask with all perseverance, not with length of words, but with the witness of groans.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Longing desire prayeth always, though the tongue be silent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou art ever longing, thou art ever praying.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When sleepeth prayer?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When desire groweth cold.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then let us beg for these eternal blessings with all eager desire, let us seek for those good things with an entire earnestness, let us ask for those good things with all assurance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For those good things do profit him that hath them, they cannot harm him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But those other temporal good things sometimes profit, and sometimes harm.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Poverty hath profited many, and wealth hath harmed many; a private life hath profited many, and exalted honour hath harmed many.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And again, money hath profiled some, honourable distinction hath profited some; profited them who use them well; but from those who use them ill, the not withdrawing them hath harmed them more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so, Brethren, let us ask for those temporal blessings too, but in moderation, being sure that if we do receive them, He giveth them, who knoweth what is expedient for us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast asked, and what thou hast asked, hath not been given thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Trust thy Father, who would give it thee, were it expedient for thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo! judge in this case by thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For such as thy son who knows not the ways of men is in regard to thee, such in regard to the Lord art thou thyself, who knowest not the things of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, thy son cries a whole day before thee, that thou wouldest give him a knife, or a sword; thou dost refuse to give it him, thou wilt not give it, thou disregardest his tears, lest thou shouldest have to bewail his death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him cry, and beat himself, or throw himself upon the ground, that thou mayest set him on horseback; thou wilt not do it, because he does not know how to govern the horse, he may throw and kill him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whom thou refusest a part, thou art reserving the whole.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that he may grow up, and possess the whole in safety, thou givest him not that little thing which is full of peril to him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so, Brethren, we say, pray as much as ye are able.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Evils abound, and God hath willed that evils should abound.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would that evil men did not abound, and then evils would not abound.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Bad times! troublesome times! this men are saying.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let our lives be good; and the times are good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We make our times; such as we are, such are the times.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what can we do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We cannot, it may be, convert the mass of men to a good life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But let the few who do give ear live well; let the few who live well endure the many who live ill.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are the corn, they are in the floor; in the floor they can have the chaff with them, they will not have them in the barn.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them endure what they would not, that they may come to what they would.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore are we sad, and blame we God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Evils abound in the world, in order that the world may not engage our love.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Great men, faithful saints were they who have despised the world with all its attractions; we are not able to despise it even disfigured as it is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The world is evil, lo, it is evil, and yet it is loved as though it were good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what is this evil world?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the heavens and the earth, and the waters, and the things that are therein, the fish, and birds, and trees, are not evil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All these are good: but it is evil men who make this evil world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet as we cannot be without evil men, let us, as I have said, whilst we live pour out our groans before the Lord our God, and endure the evils, that we may attain to the things that are good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not find fault with the Master of the household; for He is loving to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He beareth us, and not we him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He knoweth how to govern what He made; do what He hath bidden, and hope for what He hath promised.
